title: cache.refresh() silently succeeds for expired entries
description:
File: python/src/lichen/loadng/cache.py
Lines: 87-95

The `refresh` method extends a route's validity:
```python
def refresh(self, destination: IPv6Address | str, now: int) -> bool:
    dest = to_ipv6(destination)
    entry = self._entries.get(dest)
    if entry is None:
        return False
    entry.valid_until = now + self.route_timeout_ms
    self._entries.move_to_end(dest)
    return True
```

Problem: The method does not check if the entry is already expired. It will happily refresh an expired entry, bringing a 'dead' route back to life.

Scenario:
1. Route expires at t=1000
2. At t=2000, caller calls refresh(dest, 2000)
3. Entry exists (not yet cleaned by expire_old)
4. Route now valid until t=302000
5. An expired route is now active again

Impact: Stale routes can be accidentally revived if traffic happens to pass through before expire_old runs.

Fix: Check expiry before refreshing:
```python
def refresh(self, destination: IPv6Address | str, now: int) -> bool:
    dest = to_ipv6(destination)
    entry = self._entries.get(dest)
    if entry is None:
        return False
    if entry.valid_until <= now:
        # Already expired, don't revive
        del self._entries[dest]
        return False
    entry.valid_until = now + self.route_timeout_ms
    self._entries.move_to_end(dest)
    return True
```
close_reason: Implemented expiry check in refresh(); no longer revives stale routes; verified with unit test and existing test suite; committed c95a91237
